marketFocus.focus: Tech Sell-Off Continues, Silver Plunges, US-Iran Geopolitical Uncertainty
headlines: Tech sell-off continues with semiconductors under pressure (AMD -10.3%, ARM -8.6%, Qualcomm -10.3%)
Silver plunges double-digits (-13%), commodities broadly pressured overnight with spot gold -1.1%
US-Iran nuclear talks flip-flop creates geopolitical uncertainty—crude volatile (WTI settled +$1.93 at $65.14)
Trump says not much doubt interest rates will be lowered, thinks Warsh wants to cut rates anyway
marketFocus.macroContext: Markets navigate heightened risk-off environment as tech sell-off intensifies (Alphabet -2.0%, ARM -8.6%, Qualcomm -10.3%) and commodities collapse (silver -13%, gold -1.1%). Geopolitical uncertainty escalates with US-Iran nuclear talks flip-flopping—initially cancelled, then back on for Friday in Oman. APAC stocks pressured (Nikkei -0.8%, Shanghai -0.7%, ASX -0.4%) following Wall Street tech rout. European equity futures indicate modest gains (+0.2% Euro Stoxx 50). DXY +0.2% at 97.812 as USD gains against all G10 peers. Treasury futures +2.5 ticks, 10yr yield 4.268%. Key focus today: ECB Policy Announcement (15:15), BoE Policy Announcement (14:00), US Jobless Claims (15:30).

tradingSummary: Dollar gains against all G10 peers with EUR the relative outperformer, albeit lower. DXY +0.2% at 97.812, retesting Monday highs. USD/JPY +0.1% at 156.94, EUR/USD -0.2% at 1.1117, GBP/USD -0.2% at 1.3608. Commodities pressured: Silver -13%, Gold -1.1%, LME Copper -0.5%, WTI -2.1%, Brent -2.1% (despite initial rally on US-Iran talk cancellation). APAC stocks mostly lower: Nikkei 225 -0.8%, Hang Seng -0.2%, Shanghai Comp -0.7%, ASX 200 -0.4%. T-note futures +2.5 ticks, US 10yr yield 4.268%, Bund -1 tick, German 10yr yield 2.866%. Crypto pressured: Bitcoin -3.6%, Ethereum -3.3%. Risk sentiment: Risk-Off detected.

redFolderNews.event: BoE MPC Official Bank Rate Votes

USD.summary: USD gains against all G10 peers as DXY retests Monday highs near 97.81. ISM Services topped forecasts at 53.8 but ADP employment missed. Geopolitical uncertainty around US-Iran talks supports safe-haven bid. All G10 FX in red vs USD.
headlines: DXY +0.2% at 97.812, retesting Monday highs
All G10 currencies lower vs USD despite mixed data
ISM Services PMI 53.8 vs exp 53.5, ADP Employment missed
Trump says not much doubt rates will be lowered, Warsh wants cuts

EUR.summary: EUR weakens but remains relative outperformer among G10. EUR/USD -0.2% at 1.1117. Eurozone inflation data softer (Flash CPI 1.7% vs 2.0% prior, Core 2.2% vs 2.3% prior). ECB policy announcement today (15:15) is key catalyst—Main Refinancing Rate expected at 2.15%.
headlines: EUR/USD -0.2% at 1.1117, relative G10 outperformer
ECB policy announcement today 15:15—Main Refinancing Rate exp 2.15%
Flash CPI 1.7% vs 2.0% prior, Core 2.2% vs 2.3% prior
German Factory Orders ahead Thursday

GBP.summary: GBP under pressure ahead of BoE policy announcement. GBP/USD -0.2% at 1.3608. BoE expected to hold rates at 3.75% with MPC vote split (7 unchanged, 2 cuts). BoE MPR and Bailey speech (14:30) are key risk events today.
headlines: GBP/USD -0.2% at 1.3608 ahead of BoE decision
BoE expected to hold at 3.75%, MPC split 7-2
BoE Policy Announcement 14:00, Bailey speech 14:30
New Car Sales YoY data ahead today

events.event: BoE MPC Official Bank Rate Votes

JPY.summary: JPY rangebound despite risk-off environment. USD/JPY +0.1% at 156.94. Safe-haven flows offset by BoJ policy uncertainty and weak Nikkei (-0.8%). JGB auction completed with improved bid-to-cover ratio (3.64x vs 3.14x prior). Trump comments on rate cuts add uncertainty.
headlines: USD/JPY +0.1% at 156.94, rangebound despite risk-off
Japan sells JPY 525bln 30-yr JGBs, b/c 3.64x (prev 3.14x)
Nikkei 225 -0.8% on tech sell-off spillover
Trump says Warsh wants to cut rates anyway

AUD.summary: AUD pressured after RBA held rates at 3.85% as expected but signaled dovish tilt. Building Permits -14.9% MoM (exp -5.7%) adds to weakness. Trade Balance beat at 3.37B but fails to support. ASX -0.4%, commodities weak (gold -1.1%, copper -0.5%).
headlines: RBA holds at 3.85%, dovish tilt pressures AUD
Building Permits -14.9% MoM vs exp -5.7%
Trade Balance 3.37B vs exp 3.325B—fails to support
ASX 200 -0.4%, commodities pressured

CAD.summary: CAD weakens as crude oil drops -2.1% (WTI $65.14, Brent $69.46) despite initial rally on US-Iran talk cancellation. Flip-flopping geopolitics create two-way volatility. No major CAD data today, crude correlation dominates. S&P Global Manufacturing PMI 50.4 (prev 48.6) shows expansion.
headlines: WTI -2.1%, Brent -2.1% despite US-Iran talk uncertainty
CAD pressured by crude weakness and USD strength
US-Iran talks flip-flop: cancelled, then back on for Friday
S&P Global Manufacturing PMI 50.4 vs prev 48.6

CHF.summary: CHF fails to capitalize on risk-off environment as USD dominates safe-haven flows. Retail Sales data beat (YoY 2.9% vs exp 2.5%, MoM 1.0%) but fails to provide support. procure.ch Manufacturing PMI improved to 48.8 (prev 46.4) but remains contractionary.
headlines: CHF fails to gain despite risk-off, USD dominates safe-haven flows
Swiss Retail Sales YoY 2.9% vs exp 2.5%, MoM 1.0%
procure.ch Manufacturing PMI 48.8 vs prev 46.4
Gold -1.1%, silver -13%—commodities pressured

NZD.summary: NZD under pressure after mixed labour data. Unemployment Rate rose to 5.4% (exp 5.3%), Employment Change +0.5% QoQ beat expectations but Labour Cost Index missed at 2.0% YoY (exp 2.1%). Building Permits -4.6% MoM adds to weakness. Risk-off Asia weighs, Participation Rate 70.5%.
headlines: NZD Unemployment Rate rises to 5.4% vs exp 5.3%
Building Permits -4.6% MoM, labour market mixed
Employment Change +0.5% QoQ vs exp 0.3%—beats
Labour Cost Index 2.0% YoY vs exp 2.1%—misses
